title: [skip-ci] Packit: run .packit.sh only on post-commit copr builds
body:
The podman-machine-os tests fail on anything other than the version itself. So, appending the short SHA will not work for copr rpm builds fetched in podman-machine-os PRs.
    
Ref: https://github.com/containers/podman-machine-os/pull/87#issuecomment-2659477314
    
The short SHA is actually only required in post-commit builds posted to `rhcontainerbot/podman-next` Copr, as requested by Podman-Desktop.
    
This commit limits the relevant packit action to only the post-commit job.

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *lsm5* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [lsm5] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

> The short SHA is actually only required in post-commit builds posted to rhcontainerbot/podman-next Copr, as requested by Podman-Desktop.

Why exactly do we append the short commit hash? We already have a perfectly fine working commit field in podman version that is added by the Makefile or `-X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/define.gitCommit=xxx`.
So why not just use that instead of mangling the version name.

> > The short SHA is actually only required in post-commit builds posted to rhcontainerbot/podman-next Copr, as requested by Podman-Desktop.
> 
> Why exactly do we append the short commit hash? We already have a perfectly fine working commit field in podman version that is added by the Makefile or `-X github.com/containers/podman/v5/libpod/define.gitCommit=xxx`. So why not just use that instead of mangling the version name.

1. We're not using Makefile to build binaries in rpm.
2. IIUC, `define.gitCommit` relies on detecting a git repository, doesn't it? That won't work on koji builds, and I'll need to check if it could work for copr builds with packit's copr build scripts in mind. If it does, I could conditionalize this flag for copr builds.
3. This was a request from podman desktop team for their bleeding edge testing https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/20091#issuecomment-1731584413

> IIUC, define.gitCommit relies on detecting a git repository, doesn't it? That won't work on koji builds, and I'll need to check if it could work for copr builds with packit's copr build scripts in mind. If it does, I could conditionalize this flag for copr builds.

Sure but if you can add the git commit to the version you already have the git command available so you just need to pass the right `-X ...` something for the git commit. And yes that would need to be a conditional only for the copr builds. When we build actual tags on koji we don't need the extra commit. 

IMO mangling the version seems wrong for this if we already have a purpose fit field in the version output.

@benoitf remind me please, do you absolutely need the short sha for some reason in `podman --version` itself on bleeding edge builds? Can you make do with `podman version` showing `Git Commit: $COMMIT_ID` ?
hello, AFAIK at some point the Git Commit field was not there or incorrectly reporting the sha being used.

> hello, AFAIK at some point the Git Commit field was not there or incorrectly reporting the sha being used.

@benoitf ack, `podman version` (built from source) does show the correct commit. So, I'll remove the modifications from `podman --version` in the COPR rpms.
